Let ABC be a triangle with AB != AC, circumcircle Omega, and incircle omega. Let the contact points of omega with BC, CA, and AB be D, E, and F, respectively. Let the circumcircle of AFE meet Omega at K and let the reflection of K in EF be K'. Let N denote the foot of the perpendicular from D to EF. The circle tangent to line BN and passing through B and K intersects BC again at T != B.
Let the sequence (F_n)_{n>=0} be defined by F_0 = 0, F_1 = 1, and for n >= 2, F_n = F_{n-1} + F_{n-2}. Call ABC n-tastic if BD = F_n, CD = F_{n+1}, and K, N, K', B are concyclic. Across all n-tastic triangles, let a_n denote the maximum possible value of (CT * NB) / (BT * NE). Let alpha denote the smallest real number such that for all sufficiently large n, a_(2n) < alpha. Given that alpha = p + sqrt(q) for rationals p and q, what is the remainder when p^(q^p) is divided by 99991?
